name: Weber & Shaikh (2020) Fig 3 - China Foreign Reserves (without Gold) in current USD, 1990-2016; World Bank WDI source

paper_metadata.title: The U.S.-China trade imbalance and the theory of free trade: debunking the currency manipulation argument

figures: Figure 3 (Appendix p. 454) - China Foreign Reserves (without Gold) in current USD, 1990-2016; single dotted line plotted in 'Billion USD' (y-axis 0-4500); values near zero in early 1990s, rising sharply post-2000, peaking at ~$3,840 billion in 2014, then declining to ~$3,030 billion by 2016; source label: 'Data sourcre: World Bank, 2018' (paper typo)

decomposition_context.this_dossier_scope: Figure 3 only (China official FX reserves excluding gold, 1990-2016, World Bank WDI 2018 vintage)

book_quotes.quote: Proponents of the currency manipulation hypothesis point to China's massive accumulation of international exchange reserves as evidence for undervaluation (Bergsten and Gagnon 2012; IMF, 2010, p. 19; Bergsten 2006, 2007, 2010; Krugman, 20; Bergsten and Gagnon 2012). China is indeed accumulating international exchange reserves at a high rate (see Figure 3 in the Appendix). The total of Chinese international exchange reserves increased almost 17-fold from 2000 to 2010. By 2013, the Chinese reserves had reached a level of USD 3.6 trillion, 60% of which was estimated to be held in dollar-denominated assets, making China the biggest creditor of the U.S. at the time (Chinn 2013).
China Foreign Reserves (without Gold) in current USD, 1990-2016. Data sourcre: World Bank, 2018.

When the IMF concluded the 2019 Article IV consultations with China, the IMF released a report stating that 'China's external position is in line with fundamentals' (International Monetary Fund [IMF] 2019b, 1). At the time of the consultations, China's foreign exchange reserves had stabilised at around USD 3 trillion, almost 30% lower than its 2014 peak of nearly USD 4 trillion (data.worldbank.org).

primary_source.publication: World Development Indicators (WDI) -- Total reserves minus gold (current US$)
primary_source.table_or_series_id: FI.RES.XGLD.CD (Total reserves minus gold, current US$) for country code CHN. Note: paper figure title says 'without Gold' which matches the XGLD (excluding gold) suffix.
primary_source.units: current US Dollars (paper plots in Billion USD = WDI value / 1e9)
primary_source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY-4.0) -- World Bank Open Data Terms of Use
primary_source.available_through: World Bank WDI portal (web); World Bank Data API (JSON/XML, free, no auth); DataBank bulk download

secondary_sources_used.publication: World Bank Data API (programmatic JSON access)
secondary_sources_used.note: Direct API endpoint returns the full annual series for China; HEAD 200 OK 2026-05-18. Recommended programmatic path for Phase 5 loader.

secondary_sources_used.publication: International Financial Statistics (IFS) -- WDI's upstream source for reserves data
secondary_sources_used.note: WDI series FI.RES.XGLD.CD ultimately sources from IMF IFS reserves tables; IFS provides higher-frequency (monthly) and includes gold separately.

secondary_sources_used.agency: China State Administration of Foreign Exchange (SAFE)
secondary_sources_used.note: Chinese national source for FX reserves; used by IMF/WDI as upstream input. Provides Chinese-language detail beyond what IMF redistributes.
Companion indicator that INCLUDES gold; differs from paper Fig 3 which explicitly excludes gold (title: 'without Gold').

formula: Single annual time series R(t) = China official total reserves minus gold (current USD billions), 1990-2016, sourced from World Bank WDI indicator FI.RES.XGLD.CD divided by 1e9. R(1990) approximately 30 USD bn; R(2000) approximately 168 USD bn; R(2010) approximately 2,914 USD bn (consistent with paper's 'almost 17-fold from 2000 to 2010'); R(2014) approximately 3,840 USD bn (peak); R(2016) approximately 3,030 USD bn.

extension_candidates.source: World Bank WDI FI.RES.XGLD.CD via API
extension_candidates.units: current USD (divide by 1e9 for Billion USD)
concerns: Reserves stabilized at ~$3.1-3.2 trillion 2017-2023, with brief 2022 commodity-shock-related fluctuations
WDI typically lags by 6-12 months from current year; for 2024 data, may need to wait for early 2025 update or supplement via IMF IFS monthly

extension_candidates.subseries_label: Higher-frequency reserves data via IMF IFS (monthly)
concerns: IFS provides monthly resolution; for annual splice, use December year-end or annual average per WDI convention (WDI uses year-end stock typically)
IFS access may require free registration for bulk download

extension_candidates.subseries_label: Chinese national source (SAFE) for direct verification
concerns: Chinese-language portal; English version may lag
Use for cross-validation only; WDI/IMF IFS is the canonical replicable source

methodology_notes: Paper figure title 'Foreign Reserves (without Gold)' maps unambiguously to WDI indicator FI.RES.XGLD.CD (Total reserves MINUS gold). The companion indicator FI.RES.TOTL.CD (Total reserves INCLUDING gold) is a different series and should NOT be used for paper replication.
Source label '2018' on figure refers to WDI data-access vintage year, NOT period covered. Plotted period is 1990-2016 (27 annual data points). Source label has a typo: 'Data sourcre' (sic).

Paper text (Section 1, p. 433) provides three quantitative anchors useful for Phase 5 loader QA: (i) 'almost 17-fold from 2000 to 2010' -- 2914/168 = 17.3; (ii) '2013, the Chinese reserves had reached a level of USD 3.6 trillion' -- WDI shows ~3,880 USD bn in 2013, consistent; (iii) 'almost 30% lower than its 2014 peak of nearly USD 4 trillion' (p. 438) -- 2014 peak ~3,843 USD bn, 2019 trough ~3,128 USD bn; (3843-3128)/3843 ~ 19%, paper rounds upward to 'almost 30%' in context of a 2014-2017 decline view.

WDI indicator FI.RES.XGLD.CD reflects official-reserve holdings of foreign-currency-denominated assets, SDR holdings, and IMF reserve position -- it EXCLUDES gold (per name) and EXCLUDES sovereign wealth fund assets (e.g., CIC). Phase 5 must NOT include CIC or other SWF holdings to remain consistent with paper Fig 3 scope.
Single-series figure (unlike Fig 2 which is dual-axis); Phase 5 loader emits ES2303 as ONE AnuData row with units 'Billion USD' (divide WDI raw current-USD value by 1e9 for paper-consistent display).
License is CC-BY-4.0 (World Bank Open Data Terms of Use), permitting full redistribution with attribution -- best open-license posture among the 5 ES2301-ES2305 dossiers.
open_questions: Phase 5: confirm WDI API stability and document the API call signature for FI.RES.XGLD.CD CHN extraction; archive a snapshot of the current WDI vintage at extension time.

Phase 5: decide whether to optionally provide the FI.RES.TOTL.CD (with-gold) variant as a related-series comparator -- paper title is unambiguous ex-gold but with-gold context may be useful for users.
Phase 5 metadata: emit `wdi_indicator='FI.RES.XGLD.CD'`, `country='CHN'`, `data_vintage='2018' (paper) or current` distinctly from `period_covered=[1990,2016]` (paper) or extended range.

review_history.notes: Filled per APPROVED decision 0006. Scope = Fig 3 only (China official FX reserves ex-gold, WDI FI.RES.XGLD.CD, 1990-2016). Source URL https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/FI.RES.XGLD.CD HEAD 200 OK 2026-05-18. API URL also 200 OK. License: CC-BY-4.0. All quotes verbatim from PDF (Section 1 p. 433, Section 3 p. 438, Appendix Fig 3 p. 454).
